message:
fix(types): rewrite format_sqlite_float to emulate C printf %!.15g

The previous implementation used Rust's default float formatting which
produces shortest round-trip representations that differ from SQLite's
actual output. Rewrite to match C's printf("%!.15g", f) behavior:
use 15 significant digits, switch to scientific notation when exponent
is < -4 or >= 15, and strip trailing zeros while preserving at least
one digit after the decimal point. This ensures REAL values format
identically to SQLite for parity compliance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
